root.wound: The root wound is usually childhood safety: instability, scarcity, or a caregiver who could not hold. The body learned "the floor gives way."

mods.d: Revisit the young one who felt unsafe; give them, in imagination and body, the protection that was missing.
Discharge the stored survival energy — trembling, grounding, completion of the old alarm.
Men's circles where the unheld boy is finally witnessed by grounded elders.
Bare feet on earth, warm root foods, fixed rhythms — the body re-learns "held".

sacral.wound: The sacral wound is shamed desire: pleasure punished, emotion mocked, creativity dismissed. The child concluded "wanting is dangerous."

mods.d: Return to the moment joy was shamed; hand the child explicit permission to want and feel.
Meet the protector that numbs, thank it, and let the exiled feeling-self come home.
Twelve weeks of morning pages and artist dates — re-opening the pleasure channel.
Warm baths, hip openers, dance with no witness — the body remembers flow.

solar.wound: The solar wound is broken will: a controlling or critical caregiver, punished boundaries. The child learned "my power costs me love."

mods.d: Rewire the "I am small" imprint — anchor states of capability, re-pattern the inner critic's voice.
Graduated real-world "no"s — start tiny, feel the world not end, expand.
A circle that reflects your power back until you can hold it yourself.
Kapalabhati breath, sunrise movement, one completed promise to yourself daily.

heart.wound: The heart wound is conditional love: affection tied to performance, or grief never allowed to finish. The child concluded "as I am is not enough."

mods.d: Daily practice: the adult-you tells the child-you what they needed to hear — with a hand on the heart.
Unburden the exile who carries "not enough"; soften the protectors that armor the chest.
Held space (therapy, grief circle, ritual) for the losses that were never fully cried.
Loving-kindness toward self first, rose in every form, 21-second hugs.

throat.wound: The throat wound is silencing: truth punished, ignored, or called "too much." The child learned to swallow the real voice to stay safe.

mods.d: A learnable structure for saying the unsayable — needs and feelings without attack.
Say aloud — alone at first — the sentences the child was never allowed to finish.
Revisit silencing moments and re-pattern them with the voice intact.
Daily humming, chanting, singing — the throat re-learns vibration is safe.

thirdeye.wound: The third-eye wound is denied perception: gaslighting, "that didn't happen," intuition overruled. The child stopped trusting what they saw.

mods.d: With a therapist or journal: list the times you KNEW and were right. Rebuild the evidence base.
Separate the anxious forecaster from the quiet knower — learn each one's voice.
Eight weeks of watching the mind until signal and static separate on their own.
Track hunches and outcomes; trust grows on record, not hope.

crown.wound: The crown wound is meaning cut off: a childhood where awe was absent, faith weaponized, or the sacred ridiculed. The child concluded "I am on my own in a dead universe."

mods.d: Deliberate exposure to vastness — dawn skies, great music, silence — the channel reopens by use.
Untangle the sacred from whoever misused it; grief for the faith that was broken.
Long silence lets what is larger finally get a word in.
Anonymous giving — meaning arrives through the hands faster than the head.
